ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 34658001 refers to coffee as a substance; no specific instant-coffee granule code identified. Closest parent match used.

serving.description: 1 teaspoon of granules dissolved in 200 ml hot water
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving is approximately 2 g of instant coffee granules (roughly 1 heaped teaspoon) dissolved in 200 ml of hot water to produce one cup of black coffee.

nutrient_source.title: Instant coffee, powder — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for instant coffee granules without added sugar. Input record confidence 0.95 with HPB provenance retained. The HPB Foodo database reports only the core macros, dietary fibre, sodium, calcium, and iron for this entry; detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data are not included in the HPB Foodo record for this food.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for unsweetened instant coffee granules as a dry powder. GI of 29 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for black coffee (brewed/instant, no sugar), which is the closest approved analogous food. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (29) × available carbohydrate per serving (1.75 g per 100 g × 0.02 kg serving = 0.035 g) / 100 = 0.01, rounded to 0.1 for practical reporting. The extremely low carbohydrate content renders GL clinically negligible.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (12 mg/100 g); moderate caffeine content
hypertension.guidance: Unsweetened instant coffee is low in sodium and does not contribute meaningfully to dietary sodium load; however, individuals with hypertension should limit intake to 1–2 cups per day to avoid transient caffeine-mediated blood pressure elevation, and must avoid adding sugar, non-dairy creamer, or high-sodium flavourings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate (1.75 g/100 g), no added sugar, low GI (29)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Unsweetened instant coffee has negligible carbohydrate content and a low glycemic index, producing no meaningful postprandial glucose response; it may be consumed freely by individuals with type 2 diabetes provided no sugar or sweetened creamers are added, and moderate caffeine intake (up to 3–4 cups/day) is consistent with current diabetes dietary guidelines.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat, no trans fat, low sodium; polyphenol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Unsweetened instant coffee contains no saturated fat, no trans fat, and negligible sodium, and its chlorogenic acid polyphenols are associated with modest improvements in endothelial function; individuals with cardiovascular disease may consume 1–3 cups per day without concern, avoiding added sugar, cream, or non-dairy creamers that would increase saturated fat and caloric load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of positive anti-inflammatory nutrients; caffeine-calcium interaction
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Unsweetened instant coffee is not contraindicated in rheumatoid arthritis and its chlorogenic acid content has mild anti-inflammatory properties, but it does not provide omega-3 fatty acids, meaningful calcium, or other established anti-inflammatory nutrients; excessive caffeine intake may modestly impair calcium absorption, so intake should be kept moderate (1–2 cups/day) and consumed without pro-inflammatory additives such as palm-oil-based non-dairy creamers.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Instant coffee granules (no sugar) are dehydrated coffee solids produced by brewing concentrated coffee and then removing water through spray-drying or freeze-drying, leaving behind small granules or powder that dissolve rapidly in hot water. The product contains no added sugar, no dairy, and no flavourings, making it a pure coffee source. Per 100 g of dry granules, the product provides only 11 kcal, 0.2 g protein, 0.31 g fat, and 1.75 g carbohydrate, with 1.14 g naturally occurring sugars and 0.4 g dietary fibre. Sodium is very low at 12 mg/100 g. In practice, a single serving is approximately 2 g of granules dissolved in 200 ml of hot water, yielding a cup of black coffee with fewer than 1 kcal. The beverage has a characteristic bitter, roasted flavour with moderate body and aroma. Instant coffee is widely consumed across Singapore and globally as a convenient morning or afternoon beverage. Clinically, unsweetened instant coffee has a low glycemic index (approximately 29) and negligible glycemic load, making it suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease when consumed without added sugar or cream. Its chlorogenic acid and polyphenol content confer modest antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Caffeine content is approximately 30–80 mg per cup depending on brand and preparation. The product is vegan, gluten-free, halal, and kosher-compatible.
common_user_queries: Is instant coffee suitable for diabetics?
How many calories in instant coffee?
Is black instant coffee good for weight loss?
Can I drink instant coffee if I have high blood pressure?
How much sodium is in instant coffee?
What is the glycemic index of instant coffee?
Is instant coffee good for heart health?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database with confidence 0.95 as stated in the input record. The food is a well-defined, commercially standardised product with consistent composition across brands. GI is estimated from the closest approved analogous food (black coffee, University of Sydney GI Database) and is clearly documented as an analogous estimate.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g is reported as 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source; instant coffee granules without added fat contain no meaningful saturated fat. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0: confirmed zero by product formulation. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0: naturally zero as this is a plant-derived beverage with no animal-fat components. calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 per HPB source input values; these are confirmed trace/negligible per the HPB Foodo database entry. 2. The following fields are set to -1 because the HPB Foodo Singapore database entry for instant coffee granules (no sugar) does not report these values, and cross-source substitution from USDA FoodData Central was not performed to avoid mixing source bases: monounsaturated_fat_g — fatty acid fraction not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; polyunsaturated_fat_g — fatty acid fraction not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; potassium_mg — mineral not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; vitamin_c_mg — micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; vitamin_a_mcg_rae — micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg — micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg — micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; vitamin_b3_niacin_mg — micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; vitamin_b6_mg — micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; vitamin_b12_mcg — micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; folate_mcg_dfe — micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; vitamin_d_mcg — micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; vitamin_e_mg — micronutrient not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; phosphorus_mg — mineral not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; magnesium_mg — mineral not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; zinc_mg — mineral not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; selenium_mcg — mineral not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; water_g — moisture data not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry. All -1 values reflect genuine data absence in the primary approved source (HPB Foodo Singapore) as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI of 29 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for brewed/instant black coffee; no direct GI entry exists for the dry granule form. Glycemic load per serving is clinically negligible (<0.1). 4. The rheumatoid_arthritis suitability from the input record was corrected: the input marked suitable=false but the reason text described anti-inflammatory properties; the health_context entry has been revised to reflect moderate concern with accurate clinical rationale. 5. This record is a generic beverage of international origin; no Singaporean-dish multilingual naming requirement applies (G6 not triggered). Multilingual local_names fields are null by design.
